title: I dont understand the EoE
wikitext:
[]: I don't understand the EoE Post by Kye7 » Sun Sep 11, 2011 3:39 pm This is a list of things I do not understand, please help me! This is a long list, any answer would be GREATLY appreciated. What exactly is Rei? Is she a human? I don't understand why they have clones of her, and why her arm falls off whilst talking to Gendo? Does Asuka die when the 9 Eva's attack her? I thought she killed all of them. They mention that she died, but later it shows her with an eyepatch (as if no other injuries happened) When Gendo and Rei in the room with Lillith, Ritsuko is there, and he says "the truth is" than the words stop, what did Gendo say? Also when Gendo and Rei are with Lillith, and she says "I am not a puppet" and leaves him and fuses with Lillith, how is she able to fuse with Lillith and what does this mean? All of the White Evas (9 of them)? throwing 'fake' spears at Asuka, than later getting them back, and stabbing their core with them (why?) When Rei fuses with Lillith, she turn into a gigantic being with wings. What does she do? What does this signify? What does her role play in third impact? Why do the 9 white Eva's grow many faces of Rei? When Kaworu makes a 'second body' of the gigantic Rei, how is this possible? I thought he was killed? What are all of the eyes on the tree of life? What do they represent? Why is the moon a blood color? Why does the cross (tree of life) go into her forehead? What is the meaning of the giant Rei 'holding' the moon? and the two openings in her hand? Why does she grow giant wings? Why does the giant Rei 'die'? I see her head fall off of her body, than be sliced into two. What is the signifigance of this? Why does Eva unit-01 come out of her eye? [s:3nhdqpgu]Does Asuka and Shinji live in the end (on the beach)? Why does he choke her? Why does Asuka tell him "how disgusting"[/s:3nhdqpgu] Most likley, they are the only two remaining humans on the planet, thus Adam and Eve. Once again, ANY help would be appreciated! I can't seem to find help.

[]: Post by merri » Sun Sep 11, 2011 3:57 pm 1) Rei is a clone (Rei 1, 2, and 3) made from the genetic material of Yui Ikari combined with the salvaged soul of Lilith. They have clones of her as back-up if she dies (which happens twice), and no one knows for sure why her arm falls off. 2) Asuka does die, as it is inferred when her arm is sliced in half and she is impaled by 9 Lances of Longinus. She didn't kill them all, as they regenerated using their S2 engines. At the end, she is wearing an eyepatch and a bandaged arm because when she came back from the LCL Sea, she "imagined herself" in her own heart, imagining her injuries (but by the fact that she stroked Shinji with the bandaged arm, it is unlikely it was split wide open). 3) No one knows what he said to Ritsuko, but there is much speculation. 4) She fuses with Lilith because her soul is the salvaged soul of Lilith; by returning to her Lilith form, she can instigate Third Impact. 5) The white Evas are the Production models of the Eva series, and the spears they throw are manufactured reproductions of the Spear/Lance of Longinus. By stabbing their cores, it has been speculated that they did so to release their own souls into Instrumentality, and as a very strong metaphor for sex (lance penetrating their "core"). 6) When she fuses with Lilith, she generates a global anti-A.T. field that destroys all the AT Fields of Lilith-derived beings worldwide and releases their souls into Instrumentality and their bodies dissolve into LCL. The wings are Biblical imagery, representing angels' wings. As Lilith, she plays the role of gathering all the souls in the world into her "egg": the Black Moon, which was where the Geofront was. 7) I don't know why they grow Rei faces. 8) Kaworu becomes part of Giant Rei because before instigating Third Impact, Rei absorbs Gendo's hand where the dormant Adam embryo was. It is speculated that Gendo had salvaged Adam's soul from Kaworu, and as such Kaworu-who is the human vessel of Adam-joined Rei in Instrumentality. 9) I don't know this one either. 10) The moon is a blood red color because the atmosphere and water are all tinted with LCL from the dissolved bodies, making the moon appear red (LCL is red). 11) The tree of life goes into her forehead to represent more sexual imagery; the vagina-shaped opening in her head with the eye meets the pointed tree... 12) She is holding the Black Egg, her original vessel, and gathering all the souls into it. The two openings are the openings to the Chamber of Guf, where all the souls came from, and represent more sexual imagery (vagina-shaped). She grows wings for more Biblical imagery. 13) She dies because Shinji rejects Instrumentality, and her death returns all the souls to the Earth. I think the head splitting is just for added gore. 14) Because it has more dramatic squick effect. 15) Yes, in the end they are both alive because Shinji rejects Instrumentality and Asuka does later (speculated to be a lot later, due to the rusty nails). He chokes her either as vengeance, or, the more accepted reason is to check if he is still in Instrumentality dream-world or in reality. No one knows why Asuka says "how disgusting"; only speculation.

[]: Re: I don't understand the EoE Post by Jornophelanthas » Sun Sep 11, 2011 4:02 pm Kye7 wrote: Most likley, they are the only two remaining humans on the planet, thus Adam and Eve.

[]: Post by SaVaGe » Sun Sep 11, 2011 4:13 pm To gain a grasp of EoE requires intense internet trolling and self interpretation. Only so much of it is written in stone. It took me about 3 or 4 viewings and the EvaGeekWiki to realize just what I had witnessed, and even now I've yet to finish interpretting it. It's all pretty psychedelic and soul-searchy, bruh.

[]: Post by Kye7 » Sun Sep 11, 2011 4:25 pm merri wrote: 1) Rei is a clone (Rei 1, 2, and 3) made from the genetic material of Yui Ikari combined with the salvaged soul of Lilith. At the end, she is wearing an eyepatch and a bandaged arm because when she came back from the LCL Sea, she "imagined herself" in her own heart, imagining her injuries (but by the fact that she stroked Shinji with the bandaged arm, it is unlikely it was split wide open). 3) No one knows what he said to Ritsuko, but there is much speculation. 4) She fuses with Lilith because her soul is the salvaged soul of Lilith; by returning to her Lilith form, she can instigate Third Impact. 5) The white Evas are the Production models of the Eva series, and the spears they throw are manufactured reproductions of the Spear/Lance of Longinus. []: Post by TehDonutKing » Sun Sep 11, 2011 4:34 pm What everyone else said, plus this: The line translated as “How disgusting” is “kimochi warui”. I'm not sure what kimochi means, but warui means bad/evil. The phrase itself can be translated as the following: 1. “How disgusting.” 2. “I feel sick.” 3. “I feel terrible.” 4. “I feel disgusting.” 5. “How terrible.” Among other translations that don't immediately come to mind. Asuka's voice actress, Yuko Miyamura, who wrote the line, said it referred to Shinji masturbating to her. Instrumentality is the fusion of all souls into one being, similar to a technological singularity. No idea about the sexy stuff. Instrumentality was never completed, so anyone who ever lived can reform from the LCL sea, as the bodies of Lilin are composed of LCL. The Giant Impact refers to the scientific theory that moon was formed when another object collided with Earth. A newspaper article shown briefly in one episode explains that this is the First Impact. The individual computers that make up the Magi are modeled after different aspects of Naoko's personality.

[]: Post by gatotsu911 » Sun Sep 11, 2011 4:34 pm What's an Eva? Is that, like, a Freudian thing, or... am I real?

[]: Post by merri » Sun Sep 11, 2011 4:36 pm Instrumentality was a plan put into place by SEELE and the Human Instrumentality Committee to end all individuality and suffering by instigating the Third Impact. All the souls in the world would combine to form one and there would be no more suffering, hatred, love, or anything really. It was predicted in the Secret Dead Sea Scrolls. The sex references were mostly put in by Anno because he was bored? It's really debatable. They came back to life by imagining themselves and separating their souls from the LCL soup. After Lilith died people-even dead ones-could come back to life by splitting their soul from the bunch and reforming from LCL. Third Impact brought all the souls out, so I guess that is a "special circumstance". First Impact was when the moon was formed. It was when Lilith and her Black moon accidentally crashed into the planet. While Adam and his White Moon were originally there, Lilith's accidental introduction caused Adam's Spear of Longinus to activate and keep him in stasis until the Second Impact. The MAGI were programed to have the same neural thought patterns and emotions as Ritsuko's mother Naoko, not her actual brain. Eva are the Evangelion series, giant biomechanical beasts formed by salvaging the soul of the pilot's mother and combining it with the DNA of Adam or Lilith (Unit 01 only). Eva comes from the biblical story of Adam and Eve-Eva being the Hebrew word for Eve.

[]: Re: I don't understand the EoE, Join the Club. BP Post by Alaska Slim » Sun Sep 11, 2011 4:52 pm Kye7 wrote: When Rei fuses with Lillith, she turn into a gigantic being with wings. What does she do? What does this signify? What does her role play in third impact? What exactly is Rei? Is she a human? I don't understand why they have clones of her, and why her arm falls off whilst talking to Gendo? Also when Gendo and Rei are with Lillith, and she says "I am not a puppet" and leaves him and fuses with Lillith, how is she able to fuse with Lillith and what does this mean? What is the meaning of the giant Rei 'holding' the moon? and the two openings in her hand? Why does she grow giant wings? Why does the giant Rei 'die'? I see her head fall off of her body, than be sliced into two. What is the signifigance of this? Why do the 9 white Eva's grow many faces of Rei?

[]: Post by TehDonutKing » Sun Sep 11, 2011 4:53 pm Eva is NOT the Hebrew word for Eve. It's her Latin name. Her Hebrew name is Chaûoh. The Ch is pronounced like a Ch in German, the a and o are short, the u is long.

[]: Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Sep 11, 2011 4:54 pm The eyes on the tree of life could be a shout-out to the description of angels in the book of Ezekeiel, or a cognate source, plundered for Anno's pedantry.

[]: Post by Mystere9 » Sun Sep 11, 2011 5:01 pm Is there definitive proof that those are all sex references? I agree that the doors of guf in her hands look like vulvas, and I agree that the tree of life entering her forehead like that and maybe even eva popping out of her eye later look pretty sexual, but the piercing of the cores? It all seems a bit excessive if Anno really felt it necessary to include like 20 sex references in total.

[]: Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Sep 11, 2011 5:08 pm Sex and death, the two primal drivers. Also, for people who have only seen the Vaseline-o-vision Manga release, this thread may be an eye-opener http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=395 And in before Reichu's harpy with foreskin gif.

[]: Post by TehDonutKing » Sun Sep 11, 2011 5:26 pm Mystere9 wrote: Is there definitive proof that those are all sex references? I agree that the doors of guf in her hands look like vulvas, and I agree that the tree of life entering her forehead like that and maybe even eva popping out of her eye later look pretty sexual, but the piercing of the cores? It all seems a bit excessive if Anno really felt it necessary to include like 20 sex references in total.

[]: Post by gatotsu911 » Sun Sep 11, 2011 6:20 pm TehDonutKing wrote: Eva is NOT the Hebrew word for Eve. It's her Latin name. Her Hebrew name is Chaûoh. The Ch is pronounced like a Ch in German, the a and o are short, the u is long.

[]: Post by symbv » Sun Sep 11, 2011 7:42 pm Just two things I want to add: merri wrote: 15) Yes, in the end they are both alive because Shinji rejects Instrumentality and Asuka does later (speculated to be a lot later, due to the rusty nails).

[]: Post by gatotsu911 » Sun Sep 11, 2011 8:44 pm She didn't write it, exactly, the story goes that Anno asked her something like "If a stranger snuck into your bedroom at night and masturbated while watching you sleep, and he could have killed you the entire time but he didn't, what would you say?" and that was her answer. Of course, the line is also an echo of another line by Asuka in 22' and by Shinji in something like the first episode, so one wonders if maybe the use of that line was not quite as spontaneous as that story suggests.

[]: Post by thewayneiac » Sun Sep 11, 2011 8:54 pm symbv wrote: Also, where was it said that Yuko Miyamura wrote the line Kimochi Warui?

[]: Post by symbv » Sun Sep 11, 2011 9:03 pm ^ So the dialogue was not "kimochi warui" until the after-recording session which is way deep into the post-production and was only born by the failure of Miyamura coming up with the right voice for the dialogue originally stated in the script "Anta nankani korosareru nowa mappira yo!". (and it was only finally confirmed in 2005, by then I was not paying much attention to Eva any more :p ) Thanks for clearing up!

[]: Post by Fireand'chutes77 » Sun Sep 11, 2011 9:52 pm gatotsu911 wrote: What's an Eva? Is that, like, a Freudian thing, or... am I real?

11, 2011 8:44 pm She didn't write it, exactly, the story goes that Anno asked her something like "If a stranger snuck into your bedroom at night and masturbated while watching you sleep, and he could have killed you the entire time but he didn't, what would you say?" and that was her answer. Of course, the line is also an echo of another line by Asuka in 22' and by Shinji in something like the first episode, so one wonders if maybe the use of that line was not quite as spontaneous as that story suggests.
